cases.question: What major events are classified as Tropical Cyclones, and which of them have related incidents?
cases.generated_sparql:
PREFIX : <https://sakuna.ph/>
PREFIX org: <https://sakuna.ph/org/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>
PREFIX prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX cur: <http://qudt.org/vocab/currency/>
SELECT ?event ?eventName (COUNT(?incident) AS ?numberOfRelatedIncidents)
WHERE {
  ?event a :MajorEvent ;
         :hasDisasterType ?dtype .
  # Identify the Tropical Cyclone type node
  ?dtype rdfs:label "Tropical Cyclone" . 
  # Find the related incidents
  ?event :hasRelatedIncident ?incident .

  OPTIONAL { ?event :eventName ?eventName }
}
GROUP BY ?event ?eventName
HAVING (COUNT(?incident) > 0)

cases.question: Which Flash Flood or Riverine Flood events occurred, what locations did they affect, and which regions contain those locations?
cases.generated_sparql:
PREFIX : <https://sakuna.ph/>
PREFIX org: <https://sakuna.ph/org/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>
PREFIX prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX cur: <http://qudt.org/vocab/currency/>

SELECT ?event ?eventName ?locationLabel ?regionName
WHERE {
  # Match the Disaster Event and its type
  ?event a :DisasterEvent ;
         :hasDisasterType ?dtype .

  # Filter for the specific disaster type instances (Flash Flood or Riverine Flood)
  ?dtype rdfs:label ?typeName .
  FILTER (REGEX(?typeName, "Flash Flood|Riverine Flood", "i"))

  # Find the affected location of the event
  ?event :hasLocation ?location .

  # Get the label/name of the affected location (e.g., Municipality/City)
  ?location rdfs:label ?locationLabel .

  # Traverse the geographical hierarchy from the location up to the Region
  ?location :isPartOf* ?region . # Use transitive property for safety
  ?region a :Region ;
          rdfs:label ?regionName .

}

cases.question: How many fire, transport, and armed conflict events or incidents were recorded in 2022 in Central Luzon and CALABARZON?
cases.generated_sparql:
PREFIX : <https://sakuna.ph/>
PREFIX org: <https://sakuna.ph/org/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>
PREFIX prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX cur: <http://qudt.org/vocab/currency/>
SELECT (COUNT(?item) AS ?TotalCount)
WHERE {
  # Identify the entity being counted (either a major event or an incident)
  { ?item a :DisasterEvent . }
  UNION
  { ?item a :Incident . }

  # Temporal Constraint: Must have started in 2022
  ?item :startDate ?startDate .
  FILTER (?startDate >= "2022-01-01T00:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime && ?startDate < "2023-01-01T00:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime)

  # Geographic Constraint: Location must be Central Luzon or CALABARZON
  ?item :hasLocation ?loc .
  # Assuming the location entity has a label describing its region
  ?loc rdfs:label ?locationLabel .
  FILTER (CONTAINS(?locationLabel, "Central Luzon") || CONTAINS(?locationLabel, "CALABARZON"))

  # Type Constraint: Fire, Transport, or Armed Conflict
  ?item :hasDisasterType ?dtype .
  # Assuming the disaster type has a label describing its category
  ?dtype rdfs:label ?typeLabel .
  FILTER (CONTAINS(?typeLabel, "Fire") || CONTAINS(?typeLabel, "Transport") || CONTAINS(?typeLabel, "Armed Conflict"))
}
# Removed GROUP BY since we are counting all matches and only projecting the total count.

cases.question: How many evacuation centers were recorded for Tropical Cyclone events in CALABARZON during 2021, grouped by source?
cases.generated_sparql:
PREFIX : <https://sakuna.ph/>
PREFIX org: <https://sakuna.ph/org/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>
PREFIX prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX cur: <http://qudt.org/vocab/currency/>
SELECT ?source (COUNT(?center) AS ?totalEvacuationCenters)
WHERE {
  # Match the Disaster Event
  ?event a :DisasterEvent ;
         :hasDisasterType ?tc_dtype ;
         :hasLocation ?loc ;
         :startDate ?startDate .

  # Filter by Type (Tropical Cyclone) and Location (CALABARZON)
  FILTER (?tc_dtype = :TropicalCyclone)
  ?loc rdfs:label ?regionName . # Assuming the label contains CALABARZON
  FILTER (CONTAINS(?regionName, "CALABARZON"))

  # Filter by Time Period (2021)
  FILTER (?startDate >= "2021-01-01T00:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime && ?startDate < "2022-01-01T00:00:00"^^xsd:dateTime)

  # Link to Affected Population and count the centers
  ?event :hasAffectedPopulation ?ap .
  ?ap :evacuationCenters ?center .

  # Identify the data source (Provenance)
  ?event prov:wasAttributedTo ?source .

}
GROUP BY ?source
ORDER BY DESC(?totalEvacuationCenters)

  ?event a :DisasterEvent ;

  # Filter to ensure it's part of the Tropical Cyclone lineage
  ?dtype skos:broader* ?TCye .

SELECT ?event ?eventName (COUNT(?casualty) AS ?countOfAttributedDeaths)
WHERE {
  ?event a :DisasterEvent ;
         :hasCasualties ?casualty .
  # Link the specific casualty record (which includes death details)
  ?casualty a :Casualties ;
            :isOfCasualtyType "dead" ;
            # FIX: Replaced placeholder with a syntactically valid, absolute URI belonging to the ontology.
            :casualtyCause org:GovernmentResponseFailure . 
  # Bind the event details
  OPTIONAL { ?event :eventName ?eventName . }
}
GROUP BY ?event ?eventName
HAVING (COUNT(?casualty) > 0) # Ensures at least one death occurred with this specific cause

cases.question: Which province has the best disaster response program?
cases.generated_sparql:
PREFIX : <https://sakuna.ph/>
PREFIX org: <https://sakuna.ph/org/>
PREFIX rdf: <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#>
PREFIX owl: <http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#>
PREFIX skos: <http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#>
PREFIX prov: <http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#>
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX qudt: <http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/>
PREFIX cur: <http://qudt.org/vocab/currency/>
SELECT ?provinceName (SUM(?amount) AS ?TotalResponseValue)
WHERE {
  # Match a disaster event
  ?event a :DisasterEvent .

  # The event must have occurred in a specific location (the Province)
  ?event :hasLocation ?locationEntity .
  ?locationEntity a :Province ; rdfs:label ?provinceName .

  # The event must have received assistance
  ?event :hasAssistance ?assistance .
  
  # Link the assistance to a quantifiable amount (interpreting 'best response' as highest monetary aid)
  ?assistance :contributionAmount ?amount .

}
GROUP BY ?provinceName
HAVING (COUNT(?event) > 0) # Ensure the province had events with assistance recorded
ORDER BY DESC(?TotalResponseValue)
LIMIT 1
